title: Frequency of each element in the array

content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN and an array containing NNN integers.

For each element in the array you have to output it's frequency in the array.

Frequency of an element in the array tells how many times it occurs in the array.

Iterate over each element in the array and count the frequency of that element using another loop then print the frequency for each element in the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN denoting the number of elements in the array.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers, denoting the elements in the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space separated integers denoting the frequency of each element of the array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers Ai (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 10).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N space‑separated integers on a single line, where the i‑th integer is the frequency of Ai in the whole array of that test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: frequencies are 1→4, 2→3, 3→2, 4→1, so the output lists these counts in the original order. Test case 2: all five numbers are 1, therefore each position prints 5. Test case 3: 1 appears 3 times and 2 appears 2 times.
Only one element exists, its frequency is 1.
Number 2 occurs three times, 3 occurs once.

solution.approach: For each test case we count how many times each possible value (1‑10) appears. Because the value range is tiny we can use a fixed size array freq[11] initialized to zero. First pass: read the N numbers, store them in a vector/array and increment freq[value]. Second pass: iterate over the stored numbers and output freq[value] for each position, preserving the original order. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space (the frequency array).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, overall O(T * N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) to store the input array plus O(1) for the frequency array (size 11).
